commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c7cf1c351c236d175dfe7ae00fbb976ba1d8e2f1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR gates the Google Gemini CLI harness provider/backend behind `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS` and updates related setup tests, provider copy, and a Google provider rebuild doc.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +97, Docs +45. Total +160 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and `v2026.6.11` register the Gemini CLI provider/backend by default, while the PR diff gates both registration paths behind a new env var. I did not run a live onboarding or upgrade flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility/product decision about changing a shipped Google CLI provider default, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: feat(google): gate Gemini CLI runtime harness behind explicit opt-in
target.body:
## Summary

Continues the Google provider rebuild by moving Gemini CLI runtime harness registration out of the bundled Google plugin's default path.

- adds `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS` as an explicit opt-in policy
- keeps the bundled Google plugin on the official `google` provider path by default
- registers the bundled `google-gemini-cli` provider/backend only when the opt-in flag is enabled
- preserves the Google setup entry and setup-registry CLI backend default for compatibility
- updates Google API key wizard copy so the default Google group is API-key only
- adds regression tests for default-disabled and explicit-enabled bundled plugin behavior
- preserves upstream Gemini CLI backend auth-bridge tests
- documents the intended Google provider rebuild boundary and follow-up work

## Compatibility direction

The setup-registry default for `google-gemini-cli` should remain available so existing setup-only backend resolution continues to work. The explicit opt-in applies to direct bundled runtime registration only.

## Follow-up scope

- Move Gemini CLI OAuth onboarding metadata out of the default Google API-key path.
- Add a first-class Gemini API provider implementation for the current official API surface.
- Keep Vertex AI separate from AI Studio / Gemini API key auth.
- Add regression tests proving normal Google API-backed setup stays on the intended route.

## Test plan

Pending rerun after restoring the Google setup compatibility path.

Recommended targeted check:

```bash
pnpm vitest run extensions/google/gemini-cli-harness-policy.test.ts extensions/google/index-harness-registration.test.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
